docs.title: Welcome to My Portfolio
docs.text:
Arockiaraj Rayappan

Technical Engineering Lead Baton-Ubixi Systems India Private Limited, Chennai

Download CV 💡 Interests
  • 🚀 Exploring new technologies and automation
  • 📖 Technical blogging and knowledge sharing
  • 🎤 Speaking at developer communities
  • 🤖 AI and Data Engineering advancements
  • 📈 Process optimization and automation
  • 💻 Open-source contributions
About Me

A result-driven and proven Technical Engineering Lead with 11+ years of experience in FinTech (FX and Collateral Management). Specialized in building configurable and reusable products. Passionate about design, documentation, automation, and mentorship.

Roles and Responsibilities

My entire journey has been with start-ups, allowing me to take on multiple roles and responsibilities. Each role has contributed to my expertise in software development, leadership, product strategy, and automation.

  • Software Engineer: Began as an individual contributor, honing core development skills and progressively advancing to a team lead role.
  • Technical Leader: Led teams in architectural design, strategic decision-making, and delivering high-quality, scalable projects.
  • Architect: Designed and implemented scalable, reusable, and configurable software solutions to improve efficiency and maintainability.
  • Data Engineer: Built and deployed robust data pipelines using AWS services such as Glue, Lambda, DataBrew, and QuickSight.
  • Mentor: Trained and guided freshers, fostering a knowledge-sharing culture and accelerating their technical and professional growth.
  • Development Manager: Aligned team objectives with project goals, driving productivity, collaboration, and long-term career success.
  • Product Manager: Engaged with clients to gather requirements, define product vision and roadmaps, and ensure customer satisfaction.
  • Automation Tester: Developed an AI-driven testing agent to automate test generation and execution, enhancing software reliability.
Skills
  • Languages: Java, Python
  • Frameworks: Spring Boot, Microservices
  • Cloud Tools: AWS (Glue, Lambda, DataBrew, QuickSight)
  • Databases & Big Data: MySQL, NumPy, PySpark, Pandas
  • Messaging & APIs: Apache Camel, ActiveMQ, IBM MQ, Proto3, gRPC
  • DevOps & CI/CD: SonarQube, Jenkins, Prometheus, Grafana, Postman
Experience Technical Engineering Lead

📍 Baton Ubixi Systems India Pvt. Ltd. (May 2021 - Present)

  • Developed a seamless integration toolkit allowing GUI-based interface connections.
  • Built AWS-based data pipelines for analytics and reporting.
  • Designed a configurable asset movement engine with state management.
  • Led SSI normalization and custodian integrations.
  • Increased code quality by 40% across all services.
Senior Software Engineer

📍 Baton Ubixi Systems India Pvt. Ltd. (Nov 2019 - May 2021)

  • Developed middleware solutions for external/internal integrations.
  • Built an authentication & authorization service.
  • Created an AR application for Google Glass to validate labels.
Software Engineer

📍 Fifth Generation Technologies India Pvt. Ltd. (Mar 2016 - Oct 2019)

  • Contributed to Retail POS product development.
  • Created multiple billing and reporting formats.
Certifications
  • SWIFT Message – Udemy
  • Data Engineering – Udemy
Education
  • 🎓 Master of Computer Applications (MCA) – SRM University, Chennai
  • 🎓 Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA) – Bharathidasan University, Tiruchirappalli
Contact Me

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page provides detailed documentation for the API endpoints available in this project.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All API requests require authentication using an API key.

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" https://api.example.com/v1/resource

docs.title: GET /api/v1/resources
docs.text:
Returns a list of all resources.

Parameters:

Name Type Description limit integer Maximum number of resources to return offset integer Number of resources to skip

Example Response:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "1",
      "name": "Example Resource",
      "created_at": "2024-01-01T12:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "total": 100,
    "limit": 10,
    "offset": 0
  }
}

docs.title: POST /api/v1/resources
docs.text:
Creates a new resource.

Request Body:

{
  "name": "New Resource",
  "description": "A description of the resource"
}

Example Response:

{
  "id": "2",
  "name": "New Resource",
  "description": "A description of the resource",
  "created_at": "2024-05-20T14:30:00Z"
}

docs.title: Contributing Guide
docs.text: We welcome contributions to our project! This document provides guidelines for contributing to our codebase.

docs.title: Setting Up Development Environment
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Clone your fork: 
    git clone https://github.com/your-username/project-name.git
cd project-name
  3. Install dependencies: 
    pip install -r requirements.txt
  4. Install development dependencies: 
    pip install -r requirements-dev.txt

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
We follow PEP 8 style guidelines for Python code. Please ensure your code adheres to these standards.

# Run the linter
flake8 .

# Run the formatter
black .

docs.title: Pull Request Process
docs.text:
1. Create a new branch for your feature or bugfix
  2. Make your changes and commit them with clear, descriptive messages
  3. Push your branch to your fork
  4. Submit a pull request to our main repository
  5. Address any code review feedback

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
Before submitting a pull request, make sure all tests pass:

pytest

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text: If your changes require documentation updates, please include them in your pull request.

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text: Please note that this project is released with a Contributor Code of Conduct. By participating in this project, you agree to abide by its terms.

docs.title: Welcome
docs.text: Welcome to my website! This is a place where I share my thoughts, ideas, and experiences. I hope you find something interesting here.

docs.title: Understand
docs.text: First understand the problem/process what ever it is. Even understand something how it works.

docs.title: Ideate
docs.text: Think and generate multiple ideas

docs.title: Learn
docs.text: Learn/Gain the knowledge to convert your ideas into reality. By reading, watching, listening, practicing, etc.

docs.title: Implement
docs.text: Implement your ideas with your knowledge.

docs.title: Share
docs.text: Share your knowledge/experience with others. It can be in the form of writing, speaking, teaching, etc. It is a way to learn and understand better. Of course, failure experiments as well

docs.title: Collaborate
docs.text: Collaborate with others to solve the problem. It is a way to learn and understand better.

docs.title: Why Computer Programming is Important? What is the Need?
docs.text:
I took an example if Banking to explain the need for computer programming, So that every one can easily understand the need for computer programming.

Let me explain the evolution of banking, which is a great example of how technology has transformed industries.

Bank Visits: Earlier, checking balance or transferring money required visiting a bank and waiting in long queues.

ATMs: Automated Teller Machines (ATMs) allowed customers to withdraw cash, check balances, and deposit money without entering a bank.

Online Banking: Internet banking enabled users to manage accounts, transfer funds, and pay bills from anywhere using secure websites.

NEFT & RTGS: Electronic fund transfers like NEFT and RTGS allowed faster and more secure money transfers between banks.

IMPS: Immediate Payment Service (IMPS) introduced real-time 24/7 money transfers, making banking instant and seamless.

UPI: Unified Payments Interface (UPI) enabled instant transactions using mobile numbers and QR codes, simplifying digital payments.

Chat based banking: Chat-based banking services like Google Pay and PhonePe's chatbots now allow users to check balances, transfer funds, and perform banking tasks via messaging apps.

Now you can understand the need for computer programming in our daily life. It's just a simple example, but it shows how programming has transformed various aspects of our lives.

docs.title: What is Computer Programming?
docs.text:
Let say If we want to coommunicate with someone, we need to know their language. Similarly, to communicate with a computer, we need to use a programming language.

If I wanted to communicate with you, I would need to know English. Similarly, to communicate with a computer, we need to use a programming language like Python, Java, or C++.

Likewise, I have a hardware (computer), How should I tell a computer to do something? That's where programming comes in. A small and basic illustration of programming is shown below:

Programming language allows developers to create software applications, automate processes, analyze data, and build intelligent systems.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: After installation, you'll need to configure the project for your specific needs.

docs.title: Basic Configuration
docs.text:
Create a configuration file named config.yaml in your project root directory:

# Basic configuration
app_name: MyApp
debug: false
log_level: info

# Database settings
database:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
  name: mydb
  user: user
  password: password

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
You can also use environment variables to override configuration settings:

export APP_DEBUG=true
export APP_LOG_LEVEL=debug

docs.title: Advanced Options
docs.text: For advanced configuration options, please refer to the Advanced Usage section.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: This guide will walk you through the process of installing the project.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.7 or higher
  • pip (Python package manager)

docs.title: Step 1: Install via pip
docs.text: pip install your-package-name

docs.title: Step 2: Verify Installation
docs.text:
To verify that the installation was successful, run:

your-package-name --version

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Once you have successfully installed the package, you can proceed to the Configuration section to set up your environment.

docs.title: Advanced Usage
docs.text: This section covers advanced features and usage scenarios for experienced users.

docs.title: Custom Extensions
docs.text:
You can extend the functionality of the system by creating custom extensions:

from myapp.extensions import Extension

class MyCustomExtension(Extension):
    def initialize(self):
        # Extension initialization logic
        pass

    def process(self, data):
        # Custom processing logic
        return modified_data

docs.title: Performance Optimization
docs.text:
For high-traffic applications, consider these optimization techniques:

  1. Caching: Implement a caching strategy for frequently accessed data
  2. Connection Pooling: Use connection pools for database operations
  3. Asynchronous Processing: Offload heavy tasks to background workers

docs.title: Scaling Strategies
docs.text:
When scaling your application, consider:

  • Horizontal scaling for web servers
  • Vertical scaling for database servers
  • Microservice architecture for complex applications

docs.title: User Guide Overview
docs.text: This user guide provides comprehensive information about how to use the project effectively.

docs.title: Basic Concepts
docs.text:
Before diving into the details, it's important to understand the basic concepts of our system:

  • Components: The building blocks of our application
  • Workflows: How different components interact with each other
  • Configurations: How to customize the behavior of the system

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
Here are some common use cases:

  1. Setting up a development environment
  2. Creating your first project
  3. Deploying to production

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
Following these best practices will help you get the most out of our system:

  • Keep your configurations simple
  • Use version control for your projects
  • Follow the recommended project structure
